jobTitle: User Care Expert - Includes working on Saturday
jobDescription: Job Description👩🏻‍⚕️ WHAT IS ALAN? At Alan, we believe that being in good health is a basic need. However, it often remains a painful experience for us, the users. So we created Alan to make healthcare simple, accessible to all and delightful along the entire user experience. Since the beginning, we have had a very large vision for the future of healthcare that we decided to tackle step by step. We wanted to start by building the best health insurance for companies and individuals. We are on a mission to save users from the nightmares of the healthcare system. We want to empower people to make the best decisions to live a healthy life and make it easier for them to get better when they are sick. We are the first (and only) health insurance created in France with a license since 1986 and we have raised more than €35m (seed + series A) with investors such as Index Ventures. We now have more than 26,000 users and a team of 65 impressive colleagues. We have very ambitious plans for growing our business and our Care team this year - and this is only the beginning!🚀 The Challenge As a User Care Expert, you’ll be the voice of Alan towards our prospects and customers. Your first mission will be to take care of our clients’ requests in a meaningful, delightful and timely manner. Your goal is to be transparent, precise, clear and helpful to our users. We are indeed dealing with a very important topic - their health - and thriving to provide an exceptional service to them. We also expect you to help us build better customer service processes and tools and contribute to improve our product. For that, you’ll have to analyse what you see, identify areas of improvements and offer suggestions on what we can do better and faster. You’ll also take part in our growth effort by contributing to converting prospects into customers. Finally, you’ll become an Healthcare & Insurance expert and help onboard new members in the awesome Care team! There is one last thing that you need to take into account: Taking care of our client is so much at the core of our business that it is not only the job of the Care Team but it’s every Alaner’s job! We all participate in answering our customers’ requests. If you want to know more about how our Care team works: https://blog.alan.eu/pourquoi-toute-la-team-participe-au-service-client-chez-alan (in French)💚 Is it you we’re looking for? You would be a good fit at Alan if you: - Have worked with multiple clients and have a passion for taking care of them and solving their problems - Like writing (in French) and help people - Are at ease with technology and digitally savvy - Have a very empathetic and serene mindset - Are driven and entrepreneurial - Enthusiastic about teamwork, learning and teaching It’s even better if you know about the French Healthcare system! Everything else is plus. We care about having a diversity of experiences and backgrounds in our team. We are looking for candidates who are willing to work on Saturdays (and thus not work on another day from Monday to Friday in compensation). If you are open to work from Monday to Friday, please apply to the role User Expert role here: https://angel.co/l/237 Mg W⭐️ The Alan Method At Alan, we have a set of cultural values that translate into our approach to work: - We solve big problems, quickly, with focus. - We tell and share everything. - We grow as individuals while we are developing the company. We are also willing to offer Alaners a stimulating environment for people to focus on the essential: their work. Therefore, we offer: - competitive compensation packages along with meaningful equity - an awesome office in the center of Paris - relocation packages for our international candidates - daily lunch provided at the office (and snacks!) - a great healthcare program (yes, Alan Blue 💙) - a flexible and remote-friendly work environment - and many other benefits (books and trainings budget, yoga classes)- Working on Saturday can be done remotely with a lunch provided from where you work. Check out our blog for more info: https://blog.alan.eu/
